VerifierX Analyzer (VXA)

The VerifierX Analyzer (VXA) tool analyzes logs generated by VerifierX (VX) for evidence of Technical Certification Requirements (TCR) violations produced by a running game title. VXA also toggles collection of TCR data on a connected Xbox Development Kit.

Usage

USAGE: vxa [options] [sourcefile]
Option         Meaning
/Help or /? Display a usage message.
/X xboxname Specify the name or IP of a particular Xbox console.
/NoLogo Suppress display of copyright notice.
/Verbose Print extended information on failure of TCR tests.
/Clear Remove log file from Xbox Development Kit and disable collection of TCR data.
/Reset Reset log file and enable collection of TCR data.
/Log Display the entire contents of the TCR log.
/TCR:C2-8 Test whether any clock speeds have been modified by the title running on the connected Xbox console.
/TCR:C2-9 Test whether global settings have been modified.

If no TCR switch is specified, then all TCR switches are implied.

If no sourcefile is specified, VXA uses xe:\cert\VXLog.xml on the default Xbox Development Kit. The sourcefile must be surrounded by double quotes if it contains spaces. The file name of the sourcefile may be specified relative to the current working directory.

Using VXA

To collect TCR data and analyze it, follow these steps:

  1. Run the following command line to begin collecting data: 
    vxa /Reset
    VXA deletes the existing copy of xe:\cert\VXLog.xml, if any, and creates a new, empty copy.
  2. Run your game title.
  3. When you have finished running the game, run VXA again. This time, specify the TCR tests that you are interested in. For example, the following command line checks to see if the game modifies the Xbox console's clock speeds: 
    vxa /TCR:C2-8
  4. After examining the test results, turn off TCR data collection by running the following command line: 
    vxa /Clear
    VXA removes xe:\cert\VXLog.xml and shuts off data collection.
